 91→         * When formatting, seconds are always included, even if they are zero.
 92→         * Fractional parts of the second are included if non-zero.
 93→         *
 94→         * Guaranteed to parse all strings that [LocalDateTime.toString] produces.
 95→         */
 96→        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDateTime>

 99→    /**

101→     *
102→     * The components [monthNumber] and [dayOfMonth] are 1-based.
103→     *
104→     * The supported ranges of components:
105→     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
106→     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
107→     * - [monthNumber] `1..12`

127→    /**

143→    public constructor(

145→        month: Month,

148→        minute: Int,

153→    /**

155→     */

172→

174→    public val dayOfYear: Int
175→
176→    /** Returns the hour-of-day time component of this date/time value. */
177→    public val hour: Int
178→
179→    /** Returns the minute-of-hour time component of this date/time value. */
180→    public val minute: Int
181→
182→    /** Returns the second-of-minute time component of this date/time value. */
183→    public val second: Int
184→
185→    /** Returns the nanosecond-of-second time component of this date/time value. */
186→    public val nanosecond: Int
187→
188→    /** Returns the date part of this date/time value. */
189→    public val date: LocalDate
190→
191→    /** Returns the time part of this date/time value. */
192→    public val time: LocalTime
193→
194→    /**
195→     * Compares `this` date/time value with the [other] date/time value.
196→     * Returns zero if this value is equal to the other,
197→     * a negative number if this value represents earlier civil time than the other,
198→     * and a positive number if this value represents later civil time than the other.
199→     */

201→    public override operator fun compareTo(other: LocalDateTime): Int

207→     * the string representation will not include seconds. Also, fractions of seconds will add trailing zeros to
208→     * the fractional part until its length is a multiple of three.
209→     *
210→     * Examples of output:
211→     * - `2020-08-30T18:43`

214→     * - `2020-08-30T18:43:00.123456789`

217→     * @see Formats.ISO for a very similar format. The difference is that [Formats.ISO] will always include seconds,
218→     * even if they are zero, and will not add trailing zeros to the fractional part of the second for readability.
219→     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [LocalDateTime] from a string.
220→     * @see LocalDateTime.format for formatting using a custom format.

222→    public override fun toString(): String

  8→import kotlinx.datetime.format.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.UtcOffsetSerializer
 10→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * An offset from UTC.
 14→ *
 15→ * Examples of these values:
 16→ * - `Z`, an offset of zero;
 17→ * - `+05`, plus five hours;
 18→ * - `-02`, minus two hours;
 19→ * - `+03:30`, plus three hours and thirty minutes;
 20→ * - `+01:23:45`, plus one hour, 23 minutes, and 45 seconds.
 21→ */
 22→@Serializable(with = UtcOffsetSerializer::class)
 23→public expect class UtcOffset {
 24→    /**
 25→     * The number of seconds from UTC.
 26→     *
 27→     * The larger the value, the earlier some specific civil date/time happens with the offset.
 28→     */
 29→    public val totalSeconds: Int
 30→
 31→    // TODO: Declare and document toString/equals/hashCode
 32→
 33→    public companion object {
 34→        /**
 35→         * The zero offset from UTC, `Z`.
 36→         */
 37→        public val ZERO: UtcOffset
 38→
 39→        /**
 40→         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 41→         *
 42→         * Parses a string that represents a UTC offset and returns the parsed [UtcOffset] value.
 43→         *
 44→         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
 45→         *
 46→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [UtcOffset] are
 47→         * exceeded.
 48→         */
 49→        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset> = getIsoUtcOffestFormat()): UtcOffset
 50→
 51→        /**
 52→         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [UtcOffset] values.
 53→         *
 54→         * Example:
 55→         * ```
 56→         * // `GMT` on zero, `+4:30:15`, using a custom format:
 57→         * UtcOffset.Format {
 58→         *   optional("GMT") {
 59→         *     offsetHours(Padding.NONE); char(':'); offsetMinutesOfHour()
 60→         *     optional { char(':'); offsetSecondsOfMinute() }
 61→         *   }
 62→         * }
 63→         * ```
 64→         *
 65→         * Since [UtcOffset] values are rarely formatted and parsed on their own,
 66→         * instances of [DateTimeFormat] obtained here will likely need to be passed to
 67→         * [DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset.offset] in a format builder for a larger data structure.
 68→         *
 69→         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [UtcOffset.Formats].
 70→         */
 71→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 72→        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    /**
 76→     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [UtcOffset] values.
 77→     *
 78→     * See [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO], [UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC], and [UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS]
 79→     * for popular predefined formats.
 80→     *
 81→     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [UtcOffset.Format] to create a custom
 82→     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [UtcOffset] values.
 83→     */
 84→    public object Formats {
 85→        /**
 86→         * ISO 8601 extended format, which is the format used by [UtcOffset.parse] and [UtcOffset.toString].
 87→         *
 88→         * An extension of the ISO 8601 is that this format allows parsing and formatting seconds.
 89→         *
 90→         * When formatting, seconds are omitted if they are zero. If the whole offset is zero, the letter `Z` is output.
 91→         *
 92→         * Examples of UTC offsets in ISO 8601 format:
 93→         * - `Z` or `+00:00`, an offset of zero;
 94→         * - `+05:00`, five hours;
 95→         * - `-02:00`, minus two hours;
 96→         * - `-17:16`
 97→         * - `+10:36:30`
 98→         */
 99→        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
100→
101→        /**
102→         * ISO 8601 basic format.
103→         *
104→         * An extension of the ISO 8601 is that this format allows parsing and formatting seconds.
105→         *
106→         * When formatting, seconds are omitted if they are zero. If the whole offset is zero, the letter `Z` is output.
107→         *
108→         * Examples of UTC offsets in ISO 8601 basic format:
109→         * - `Z`
110→         * - `+05`
111→         * - `-1716`
112→         * - `+103630`
113→         *
114→         * @see UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS
115→         */
116→        public val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
117→
118→        /**
119→         * A subset of the ISO 8601 basic format that always outputs and parses exactly a numeric sign and four digits:
120→         * two digits for the hour and two digits for the minute. If the offset has a non-zero number of seconds,
121→         * they are truncated.
122→         *
123→         * Examples of UTC offsets in this format:
124→         * - `+0000`
125→         * - `+0500`
126→         * - `-1716`
127→         * - `+1036`
128→         *
129→         * @see UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC
130→         */
131→        public val FOUR_DIGITS: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>
132→    }
133→
134→    /**
135→     * Converts this UTC offset to the extended ISO-8601 string representation.
136→     *
137→     * @see Formats.ISO for the format details.
138→     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [UtcOffset] from a string.
139→     * @see UtcOffset.format for formatting using a custom format.
140→     */
141→    public override fun toString(): String
142→}
143→
144→/**
145→ * Formats this value using the given [format].
146→ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
147→ */
148→public fun UtcOffset.format(format: DateTimeFormat<UtcOffset>): String = format.format(this)
149→
150→/**
151→ * Constructs a [UtcOffset] from hours, minutes, and seconds components.
152→ *
153→ * All components must have the same sign.
154→ *
155→ * The bounds are checked: it is invalid to pass something other than `±[0; 59]` as the number of seconds or minutes.
156→ * For example, `UtcOffset(hours = 3, minutes = 61)` is invalid.
157→ *
158→ * However, the non-null component of the highest order can exceed these bounds,
159→ * for example, `UtcOffset(minutes = 241)` is valid.
160→ *
161→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if a component exceeds its bounds when a higher order component is specified.
162→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if components have different signs.
163→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the resulting `UtcOffset` value is outside of range `±18:00`.
164→ */
165→public expect fun UtcOffset(hours: Int? = null, minutes: Int? = null, seconds: Int? = null): UtcOffset
166→
167→@Deprecated("Use UtcOffset.ZERO instead", ReplaceWith("UtcOffset.ZERO"), DeprecationLevel.ERROR)
168→public fun UtcOffset(): UtcOffset = UtcOffset.ZERO
169→
170→/**
171→ * Returns the fixed-offset time zone with the given UTC offset.
172→ */
173→public fun UtcOffset.asTimeZone(): FixedOffsetTimeZone = FixedOffsetTimeZone(this)
174→
175→// workaround for https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-65484
176→internal fun getIsoUtcOffestFormat() = UtcOffset.Formats.ISO

1→/*
  2→ * Copyright 2019-2023 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3→ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4→ */
  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime.format
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.LocalTime
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.DateTimeFormatException
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.DecimalFraction
 11→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 12→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.Copyable
 13→
 14→/**
 15→ * The AM/PM marker that indicates whether the hour in range `1..12` is before or after noon.
 16→ */
 17→public enum class AmPmMarker {
 18→    /** The time is before noon. */
 19→    AM,
 20→
 21→    /** The time is after noon. */
 22→    PM,
 23→}
 24→
 25→internal interface TimeFieldContainer {
 26→    var minute: Int?
 27→    var second: Int?
 28→    var nanosecond: Int?
 29→    var hour: Int?
 30→    var hourOfAmPm: Int?
 31→    var amPm: AmPmMarker?
 32→
 33→    var fractionOfSecond: DecimalFraction?
 34→        get() = nanosecond?.let { DecimalFraction(it, 9) }
 35→        set(value) {
 36→            nanosecond = value?.fractionalPartWithNDigits(9)
 37→        }
 38→}
 39→
 40→private object TimeFields {
 41→    val hour = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::hour), minValue = 0, maxValue = 23)
 42→    val minute = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::minute), minValue = 0, maxValue = 59)
 43→    val second =
 44→        UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::second), minValue = 0, maxValue = 59, defaultValue = 0)
 45→    val fractionOfSecond =
 46→        GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::fractionOfSecond), defaultValue = DecimalFraction(0, 9))
 47→    val amPm = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::amPm))
 48→    val hourOfAmPm = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(TimeFieldContainer::hourOfAmPm), minValue = 1, maxValue = 12)
 49→}
 50→
 51→internal class IncompleteLocalTime(
 52→    override var hour: Int? = null,
 53→    override var hourOfAmPm: Int? = null,
 54→    override var amPm: AmPmMarker? = null,
 55→    override var minute: Int? = null,
 56→    override var second: Int? = null,
 57→    override var nanosecond: Int? = null
 58→) : TimeFieldContainer, Copyable<IncompleteLocalTime> {
 59→    fun toLocalTime(): LocalTime {
 60→        val hour: Int = hour?.let { hour ->
 61→            hourOfAmPm?.let {
 62→                require((hour + 11) % 12 + 1 == it) { "Inconsistent hour and hour-of-am-pm: hour is $hour, but hour-of-am-pm is $it" }
 63→            }
 64→            amPm?.let { amPm ->
 65→                require((amPm == AmPmMarker.PM) == (hour >= 12)) {
 66→                    "Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is $hour, but the AM/PM marker is $amPm"
 67→                }
 68→            }
 69→            hour
 70→        } ?: hourOfAmPm?.let { hourOfAmPm ->
 71→            amPm?.let { amPm ->
 72→                hourOfAmPm.let { if (it == 12) 0 else it } + if (amPm == AmPmMarker.PM) 12 else 0
 73→            }
 74→        } ?: throw DateTimeFormatException("Incomplete time: missing hour")
 75→        return LocalTime(
 76→            hour,
 77→            requireParsedField(minute, "minute"),
 78→            second ?: 0,
 79→            nanosecond ?: 0,
 80→        )
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    fun populateFrom(localTime: LocalTime) {
 84→        hour = localTime.hour
 85→        hourOfAmPm = (localTime.hour + 11) % 12 + 1
 86→        amPm = if (localTime.hour >= 12) AmPmMarker.PM else AmPmMarker.AM
 87→        minute = localTime.minute
 88→        second = localTime.second
 89→        nanosecond = localTime.nanosecond
 90→    }
 91→
 92→    override fun copy(): IncompleteLocalTime = IncompleteLocalTime(hour, hourOfAmPm, amPm, minute, second, nanosecond)
 93→
 94→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
 95→        other is IncompleteLocalTime && hour == other.hour && hourOfAmPm == other.hourOfAmPm && amPm == other.amPm &&
 96→            minute == other.minute && second == other.second && nanosecond == other.nanosecond
 97→
 98→    override fun hashCode(): Int =
 99→        (hour ?: 0) * 31 + (hourOfAmPm ?: 0) * 31 + (amPm?.hashCode() ?: 0) * 31 + (minute ?: 0) * 31 +
100→            (second ?: 0) * 31 + (nanosecond ?: 0)
101→
102→    override fun toString(): String =
103→        "${hour ?: "??"}:${minute ?: "??"}:${second ?: "??"}.${
104→            nanosecond?.let { nanos ->
105→                nanos.toString().let { it.padStart(9 - it.length, '0') }
106→            } ?: "???"
107→        }"
108→}

110→internal interface AbstractWithTimeBuilder : DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime {
111→    fun addFormatStructureForTime(structure: FormatStructure<TimeFieldContainer>)
112→
113→    override fun hour(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(HourDirective(padding)))
114→    override fun amPmHour(padding: Padding) =
115→        addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(AmPmHourDirective(padding)))
116→
117→    override fun amPmMarker(am: String, pm: String) =
118→        addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(AmPmMarkerDirective(am, pm)))
119→
120→    override fun minute(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(MinuteDirective(padding)))
121→    override fun second(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(SecondDirective(padding)))
122→    override fun secondFraction(minLength: Int, maxLength: Int) =
123→        addFormatStructureForTime(BasicFormatStructure(FractionalSecondDirective(minLength, maxLength)))
124→
125→    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
126→    override fun time(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalTime>) = when (format) {
127→        is LocalTimeFormat -> addFormatStructureForTime(format.actualFormat)
128→    }
129→}

131→private class HourDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
132→    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
133→        TimeFields.hour,
134→        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
135→        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2)
136→    ) {
137→    override val builderRepresentation: String
138→        get() = when (padding) {
139→            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::hour.name}()"
140→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::hour.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
141→        }
142→
143→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is HourDirective && padding == other.padding
144→    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
145→}

148→    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(

150→        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2)
151→    ) {

153→        get() = when (padding) {
154→            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::amPmHour.name}()"
155→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::amPmHour.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"

162→private class AmPmMarkerDirective(private val amString: String, private val pmString: String) :
163→    NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer, AmPmMarker>(
164→        TimeFields.amPm, mapOf(

191→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::minute.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"

198→private class SecondDirective(private val padding: Padding) :

203→    ) {
204→
205→    override val builderRepresentation: String
206→        get() = when (padding) {

208→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::second.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"

215→internal class FractionalSecondDirective(
216→    private val minDigits: Int,
217→    private val maxDigits: Int,
218→    zerosToAdd: List<Int> = NO_EXTRA_ZEROS,
219→) :
220→    DecimalFractionFieldFormatDirective<TimeFieldContainer>(
221→        TimeFields.fractionOfSecond,
222→        minDigits,
223→        maxDigits,
224→        zerosToAdd
225→    ) {
226→
227→    override val builderRepresentation: String
228→        get() {
229→            val ref = "secondFraction" // can't directly reference `secondFraction` due to resolution ambiguity
230→            // we ignore `grouping`, as it's not representable in the end users' code
231→            return when {
232→                minDigits == 1 && maxDigits == 9 -> "$ref()"
233→                minDigits == 1 -> "$ref(maxLength = $maxDigits)"
234→                maxDigits == 1 -> "$ref(minLength = $minDigits)"
235→                maxDigits == minDigits -> "$ref($minDigits)"
236→                else -> "$ref($minDigits, $maxDigits)"
237→            }
238→        }
239→
240→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
241→        other is FractionalSecondDirective && minDigits == other.minDigits && maxDigits == other.maxDigits
242→
243→    override fun hashCode(): Int = 31 * minDigits + maxDigits
244→
245→    companion object {
246→        val NO_EXTRA_ZEROS = listOf(0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0)
247→        val GROUP_BY_THREE = listOf(2, 1, 0, 2, 1, 0, 2, 1, 0)
248→    }
249→}

252→    AbstractDateTimeFormat<LocalTime, IncompleteLocalTime>() {
253→    override fun intermediateFromValue(value: LocalTime): IncompleteLocalTime =
254→        IncompleteLocalTime().apply { populateFrom(value) }
255→
256→    override fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: IncompleteLocalTime): LocalTime = intermediate.toLocalTime()
257→
258→    override val emptyIntermediate: IncompleteLocalTime get() = emptyIncompleteLocalTime
259→
260→    companion object {
261→        fun build(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime.() -> Unit): LocalTimeFormat {
262→            val builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
263→            builder.block()
264→            return LocalTimeFormat(builder.build())
265→        }
266→
267→    }
268→
269→    private class Builder(override val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<TimeFieldContainer>) :
270→        AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<TimeFieldContainer, Builder>, AbstractWithTimeBuilder {
271→
272→        override fun addFormatStructureForTime(structure: FormatStructure<TimeFieldContainer>) {
273→            actualBuilder.add(structure)
274→        }
275→
276→        override fun createEmpty(): Builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
277→    }

279→}

283→    LocalTimeFormat.build {
284→        hour()
285→        char(':')
286→        minute()
287→        alternativeParsing({

0→/*

  4→
  5→package kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser
  6→
  7→internal interface ParserOperation<in Output> {
  8→    fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult
  9→}
 10→
 11→/**
 12→ * Consumes exactly the string [string].
 13→ */
 14→internal class PlainStringParserOperation<Output>(val string: String) : ParserOperation<Output> {
 15→    init {
 16→        require(string.isNotEmpty()) { "Empty string is not allowed" }
 17→        require(!string[0].isDigit()) { "String '$string' starts with a digit" }
 18→        require(!string[string.length - 1].isDigit()) { "String '$string' ends with a digit" }
 19→    }
 20→
 21→    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
 22→        if (startIndex + string.length > input.length)
 23→            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Unexpected end of input: yet to parse '$string'" }
 24→        for (i in string.indices) {
 25→            if (input[startIndex + i] != string[i]) return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) {
 26→                "Expected $string but got ${input.substring(startIndex, startIndex + i + 1)}"
 27→            }
 28→        }
 29→        return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + string.length)
 30→    }
 31→
 32→    // TODO: properly escape
 33→    override fun toString(): String = "'$string'"
 34→}
 35→
 36→/**
 37→ * Greedily consumes as many digits as possible and redistributes them among [consumers].
 38→ *
 39→ * At most one consumer is allowed to have a variable length.
 40→ * If more digits are supplied than the sum of lengths of all consumers, this is the consumer that will receive the
 41→ * extra.
 42→ */
 43→internal class NumberSpanParserOperation<Output>(
 44→    val consumers: List<NumberConsumer<Output>>,
 45→) : ParserOperation<Output> {
 46→
 47→    private val minLength = consumers.sumOf { it.length ?: 1 }
 48→    private val isFlexible = consumers.any { it.length == null }
 49→
 50→    init {
 51→        require(consumers.all { (it.length ?: Int.MAX_VALUE) > 0 })
 52→        require(consumers.count { it.length == null } <= 1)
 53→    }
 54→
 55→    private val whatThisExpects: String
 56→        get() {
 57→            val consumerLengths = consumers.map {
 58→                when (val length = it.length) {
 59→                    null -> "at least one digit"
 60→                    else -> "$length digits"
 61→                } + " for ${it.whatThisExpects}"
 62→            }
 63→            return if (isFlexible) {
 64→                "a number with at least $minLength digits: $consumerLengths"
 65→            } else {
 66→                "a number with exactly $minLength digits: $consumerLengths"
 67→            }
 68→        }
 69→
 70→    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
 71→        if (startIndex + minLength > input.length)
 72→            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Unexpected end of input: yet to parse $whatThisExpects" }
 73→        var digitsInRow = 0
 74→        while (startIndex + digitsInRow < input.length && input[startIndex + digitsInRow].isDigit()) {
 75→            ++digitsInRow
 76→        }
 77→        if (digitsInRow < minLength)
 78→            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) {
 79→                "Only found $digitsInRow digits in a row, but need to parse $whatThisExpects"
 80→            }
 81→        var index = startIndex
 82→        for (i in consumers.indices) {
 83→            val length = consumers[i].length ?: (digitsInRow - minLength + 1)
 84→            val numberString = input.substring(index, index + length)
 85→            val error = consumers[i].consume(storage, numberString)
 86→            if (error != null) {
 87→                return ParseResult.Error(index) {
 88→                    "Can not interpret the string '$numberString' as ${consumers[i].whatThisExpects}: ${error.errorMessage()}"
 89→                }
 90→            }
 91→            index += length
 92→        }
 93→        return ParseResult.Ok(index)
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    override fun toString(): String = whatThisExpects
 97→}
 98→
 99→internal class SignParser<Output>(
100→    private val isNegativeSetter: (Output, Boolean) -> Unit,
101→    private val withPlusSign: Boolean,
102→    private val whatThisExpects: String,
103→) : ParserOperation<Output> {
104→    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
105→        if (startIndex >= input.length)
106→            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex)
107→        val char = input[startIndex]
108→        if (char == '-') {
109→            isNegativeSetter(storage, true)
110→            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + 1)
111→        }
112→        if (char == '+' && withPlusSign) {
113→            isNegativeSetter(storage, false)
114→            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + 1)
115→        }
116→        return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Expected $whatThisExpects but got $char" }
117→    }
118→
119→    override fun toString(): String = whatThisExpects
120→}
121→
122→/**
123→ * Consumes an empty string and unconditionally modifies the accumulator.
124→ */
125→internal class UnconditionalModification<Output>(
126→    private val operation: (Output) -> Unit
127→) : ParserOperation<Output> {
128→    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
129→        operation(storage)
130→        return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex)
131→    }
132→}
133→
134→/**
135→ * Matches the longest suitable string from `strings` and calls [consume] with the matched string.
136→ */
137→internal class StringSetParserOperation<Output>(
138→    strings: Collection<String>,
139→    private val setter: AssignableField<Output, String>,
140→    private val whatThisExpects: String,
141→) : ParserOperation<Output> {
142→
143→    // TODO: tries don't have good performance characteristics for small sets, add a special case for small sets
144→
145→    private class TrieNode(
146→        val children: MutableList<Pair<String, TrieNode>> = mutableListOf(),
147→        var isTerminal: Boolean = false
148→    )
149→
150→    private val trie = TrieNode()
151→
152→    init {
153→        for (string in strings) {
154→            var node = trie
155→            for (char in string) {
156→                val searchResult = node.children.binarySearchBy(char.toString()) { it.first }
157→                node = if (searchResult < 0) {
158→                    TrieNode().also { node.children.add(-searchResult - 1, char.toString() to it) }
159→                } else {
160→                    node.children[searchResult].second
161→                }
162→            }
163→            node.isTerminal = true
164→        }
165→        fun reduceTrie(trie: TrieNode) {
166→            for ((_, child) in trie.children) {
167→                reduceTrie(child)
168→            }
169→            val newChildren = mutableListOf<Pair<String, TrieNode>>()
170→            for ((key, child) in trie.children) {
171→                if (!child.isTerminal && child.children.size == 1) {
172→                    val (grandChildKey, grandChild) = child.children.single()
173→                    newChildren.add(key + grandChildKey to grandChild)
174→                } else {
175→                    newChildren.add(key to child)
176→                }
177→            }
178→            trie.children.clear()
179→            trie.children.addAll(newChildren.sortedBy { it.first })
180→        }
181→        reduceTrie(trie)
182→    }
183→
184→    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
185→        var node = trie
186→        var index = startIndex
187→        var lastMatch: Int? = null
188→        loop@ while (index <= input.length) {
189→            if (node.isTerminal) lastMatch = index
190→            for ((key, child) in node.children) {
191→                if (input.startsWith(key, index)) {
192→                    node = child
193→                    index += key.length
194→                    continue@loop
195→                }
196→            }
197→            break // nothing found
198→        }
199→        return if (lastMatch != null) {
200→            setter.setWithoutReassigning(storage, input.substring(startIndex, lastMatch), startIndex, lastMatch)
201→        } else {
202→            ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Expected $whatThisExpects but got ${input.substring(startIndex, index)}" }
203→        }
204→    }
205→}
206→
207→internal fun <Output> SignedIntParser(
208→    minDigits: Int?,
209→    maxDigits: Int?,
210→    spacePadding: Int?,
211→    setter: AssignableField<Output, Int>,
212→    name: String,
213→    plusOnExceedsWidth: Int?,
214→): ParserStructure<Output> {
215→    val parsers = mutableListOf(
216→        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, setter, name, withMinus = true)
217→    )
218→    if (plusOnExceedsWidth != null) {
219→        parsers.add(
220→            spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, plusOnExceedsWidth, spacePadding, setter, name)
221→        )
222→        parsers.add(
223→            ParserStructure(
224→                listOf(
225→                    PlainStringParserOperation("+"),
226→                    NumberSpanParserOperation(
227→                        listOf(
228→                            UnsignedIntConsumer(
229→                                plusOnExceedsWidth + 1,
230→                                maxDigits,
231→                                setter,
232→                                name,
233→                                multiplyByMinus1 = false
234→                            )
235→                        )
236→                    )
237→                ),
238→                emptyList()
239→            )
240→        )
241→    } else {
242→        parsers.add(
243→            spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, setter, name)
244→        )
245→    }
246→    return ParserStructure(
247→        emptyList(),
248→        parsers,
249→    )
250→}
251→
252→// With maxWidth = 4,
253→// padWidth = 7: "   " + (four digits | " " (three digits | " " (two digits | " ", one digit)))
254→// padWidth = 3: three to four digits | " " (two digits | " ", one digit)
255→internal fun <Target> spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(
256→    minDigits: Int?,
257→    maxDigits: Int?,
258→    spacePadding: Int?,
259→    setter: AssignableField<Target, Int>,
260→    name: String,
261→    withMinus: Boolean = false,
262→): ParserStructure<Target> {
263→    val minNumberLength = (minDigits ?: 1) + if (withMinus) 1 else 0
264→    val maxNumberLength = maxDigits?.let { if (withMinus) it + 1 else it } ?: Int.MAX_VALUE
265→    val spacePadding = spacePadding ?: 0
266→    fun numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength: Int, maxNumberLength: Int): ParserStructure<Target> {
267→        check(maxNumberLength >= 1 + if (withMinus) 1 else 0)
268→        return ParserStructure(
269→            buildList {
270→                if (withMinus) add(PlainStringParserOperation("-"))
271→                add(
272→                    NumberSpanParserOperation(
273→                        listOf(
274→                            UnsignedIntConsumer(
275→                                minNumberLength - if (withMinus) 1 else 0,
276→                                maxNumberLength - if (withMinus) 1 else 0,
277→                                setter,
278→                                name,
279→                                multiplyByMinus1 = withMinus,
280→                            )
281→                        )
282→                    )
283→                )
284→            },
285→            emptyList()
286→        )
287→    }
288→
289→    val maxPaddedNumberLength = minOf(maxNumberLength, spacePadding)
290→    if (minNumberLength >= maxPaddedNumberLength) return numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength, maxNumberLength)
291→    // invariant: the length of the string parsed by 'accumulated' is exactly 'accumulatedWidth'
292→    var accumulated: ParserStructure<Target> = numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength, minNumberLength)
293→    for (accumulatedWidth in minNumberLength until maxPaddedNumberLength) {
294→        accumulated = ParserStructure(
295→            emptyList(),
296→            listOf(
297→                numberOfRequiredLengths(accumulatedWidth + 1, accumulatedWidth + 1),
298→                listOf(
299→                    ParserStructure(listOf(PlainStringParserOperation(" ")), emptyList()),
300→                    accumulated
301→                ).concat()
302→            )
303→        )
304→    }
305→    // accumulatedWidth == maxNumberLength || accumulatedWidth == spacePadding.
306→    // In the first case, we're done, in the second case, we need to add the remaining numeric lengths.
307→    return if (spacePadding > maxNumberLength) {
308→        val prepadding = PlainStringParserOperation<Target>(" ".repeat(spacePadding - maxNumberLength))
309→        listOf(
310→            ParserStructure(
311→                listOf(
312→                    prepadding,
313→                ),
314→                emptyList()
315→            ), accumulated
316→        ).concat()
317→    } else if (spacePadding == maxNumberLength) {
318→        accumulated
319→    } else {
320→        val r = ParserStructure(
321→            emptyList(),
322→            listOf(
323→                numberOfRequiredLengths(spacePadding + 1, maxNumberLength),
324→                accumulated
325→            )
326→        )
327→        r
328→    }
329→}
330→
331→internal fun <Output> ReducedIntParser(
332→    digits: Int,

334→    setter: AssignableField<Output, Int>,
335→    name: String,

338→    listOf(
339→        ParserStructure(
340→            listOf(
341→                NumberSpanParserOperation(
342→                    listOf(

344→                            digits,
345→                            setter,
346→                            name,
347→                            base = base
348→                        )
349→                    )
350→                )
351→            ),
352→            emptyList()
353→        ),
354→        ParserStructure(

357→                NumberSpanParserOperation(
358→                    listOf(
359→                        UnsignedIntConsumer(
360→                            null,
361→                            null,
362→                            setter,
363→                            name,

365→                        )
366→                    )
367→                )
368→            ),
369→            emptyList()
370→        ),
371→        ParserStructure(
372→            listOf(
373→                PlainStringParserOperation("-"),
374→                NumberSpanParserOperation(
375→                    listOf(
376→                        UnsignedIntConsumer(
377→                            null,
378→                            null,
379→                            setter,
380→                            name,
381→                            multiplyByMinus1 = true
382→                        )
383→                    )
384→                )

386→            emptyList()

390→
391→internal interface AssignableField<in Object, Type> {
392→    /**
393→     * If the field is not set, sets it to the given value.
394→     * If the field is set to the given value, does nothing.
395→     * If the field is set to a different value, throws [IllegalArgumentException].
396→     *
397→     * This function is used to ensure internal consistency during parsing.
398→     * There exist formats where the same data is repeated several times in the same object, for example,
399→     * "14:15 (02:15 PM)". In such cases, we want to ensure that the values are consistent.
400→     */
401→    fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Object, newValue: Type): Type?
402→
403→    /**
404→     * The name of the field.
405→     */
406→    val name: String
407→}
408→
409→private fun <Object, Type> AssignableField<Object, Type>.setWithoutReassigning(
410→    receiver: Object,
411→    value: Type,
412→    position: Int,
413→    nextIndex: Int
414→): ParseResult {
415→    val conflictingValue = trySetWithoutReassigning(receiver, value)
416→    return if (conflictingValue === null) {
417→        ParseResult.Ok(nextIndex)
418→    } else {
419→        ParseResult.Error(position) {
420→            "Attempting to assign conflicting values '$conflictingValue' and '$value' to field '$name'"
421→        }
422→    }
423→}

 15→internal interface FieldFormatDirective<in Target> {

 19→    val field: FieldSpec<Target, *>

 33→     */

 37→/**
 38→ * A directive for a decimal format of an integer field that is known to be unsigned.
 39→ * The field is formatted with the field padded to [minDigits] with zeroes,
 40→ * and the parser expects the field to be at least [minDigits] digits long.
 41→ */
 42→internal abstract class UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
 43→    final override val field: UnsignedFieldSpec<Target>,
 44→    private val minDigits: Int,
 45→    private val spacePadding: Int?,
 46→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
 47→
 48→    private val maxDigits: Int = field.maxDigits
 49→
 50→    init {
 51→        require(minDigits >= 0) {
 52→            "The minimum number of digits ($minDigits) is negative"
 53→        }
 54→        require(maxDigits >= minDigits) {
 55→            "The maximum number of digits ($maxDigits) is less than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 56→        }
 57→        if (spacePadding != null) {
 58→            require(spacePadding > minDigits) {
 59→                "The space padding ($spacePadding) should be more than the minimum number of digits ($minDigits)"
 60→            }
 61→        }
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
 65→        val formatter = UnsignedIntFormatterStructure(
 66→            number = field.accessor::getterNotNull,
 67→            zeroPadding = minDigits,
 68→        )
 69→        return if (spacePadding != null) SpacePaddedFormatter(formatter, spacePadding) else formatter
 70→    }
 71→
 72→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
 73→        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, field.accessor, field.name)
 74→}

 88→        }

 93→            ?: "The value $it of ${field.name} does not have a corresponding string representation"
 94→    }

 96→    private inner class AssignableString: AssignableField<Target, String> {
 97→        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =
 98→            field.accessor.trySetWithoutReassigning(container, values.indexOf(newValue) + field.minValue)?.let {
 99→                values[it - field.minValue]
100→            }
101→
102→        override val name: String get() = this@NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective.name
103→    }
104→
105→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> =
106→        StringFormatterStructure(::getStringValue)
107→
108→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =
109→        ParserStructure(

112→            ), emptyList()

124→

126→

130→    }

133→        override fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Target, newValue: String): String? =

138→

141→
142→    override fun parser(): ParserStructure<Target> =

146→            ), emptyList()

151→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, String>,
152→    private val acceptedStrings: Set<String>,
153→) : FieldFormatDirective<Target> {
154→
155→    init {
156→        require(acceptedStrings.isNotEmpty()) {
157→            "The set of accepted strings is empty"
158→        }
159→    }

171→internal abstract class SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<in Target>(
172→    final override val field: FieldSpec<Target, Int>,
173→    private val minDigits: Int?,
174→    private val maxDigits: Int?,
175→    private val spacePadding: Int?,
176→    private val outputPlusOnExceededWidth: Int?,

181→        require(maxDigits == null || minDigits == null || maxDigits >= minDigits) {

183→        }
184→    }

186→    override fun formatter(): FormatterStructure<Target> {
187→        val formatter = SignedIntFormatterStructure(

190→            outputPlusOnExceededWidth = outputPlusOnExceededWidth,
191→        )

118→internal fun <T> requireParsedField(field: T?, name: String): T {
119→    if (field == null) {

125→internal interface DateFieldContainer {
126→    var year: Int?
127→    var monthNumber: Int?
128→    var dayOfMonth: Int?
129→    var isoDayOfWeek: Int?

132→private object DateFields {
133→    val year = GenericFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::year))
134→    val month = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::monthNumber), minValue = 1, maxValue = 12)
135→    val dayOfMonth = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::dayOfMonth), minValue = 1, maxValue = 31)
136→    val isoDayOfWeek = UnsignedFieldSpec(PropertyAccessor(DateFieldContainer::isoDayOfWeek), minValue = 1, maxValue = 7)

139→/**

141→ */
142→internal class IncompleteLocalDate(
143→    override var year: Int? = null,
144→    override var monthNumber: Int? = null,
145→    override var dayOfMonth: Int? = null,
146→    override var isoDayOfWeek: Int? = null
147→) : DateFieldContainer, Copyable<IncompleteLocalDate> {
148→    fun toLocalDate(): LocalDate {
149→        val date = LocalDate(
150→            requireParsedField(year, "year"),
151→            requireParsedField(monthNumber, "monthNumber"),
152→            requireParsedField(dayOfMonth, "dayOfMonth")
153→        )
154→        isoDayOfWeek?.let {
155→            if (it != date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber) {
156→                throw DateTimeFormatException(
157→                    "Can not create a LocalDate from the given input: " +
158→                        "the day of week is ${DayOfWeek(it)} but the date is $date, which is a ${date.dayOfWeek}"
159→                )
160→            }
161→        }
162→        return date
163→    }
164→
165→    fun populateFrom(date: LocalDate) {
166→        year = date.year
167→        monthNumber = date.monthNumber
168→        dayOfMonth = date.dayOfMonth
169→        isoDayOfWeek = date.dayOfWeek.isoDayNumber
170→    }
171→
172→    override fun copy(): IncompleteLocalDate = IncompleteLocalDate(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, isoDayOfWeek)
173→
174→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
175→        other is IncompleteLocalDate && year == other.year && monthNumber == other.monthNumber &&
176→            dayOfMonth == other.dayOfMonth && isoDayOfWeek == other.isoDayOfWeek
177→
178→    override fun hashCode(): Int =
179→        year.hashCode() * 31 + monthNumber.hashCode() * 31 + dayOfMonth.hashCode() * 31 + isoDayOfWeek.hashCode() * 31
180→
181→    override fun toString(): String =
182→        "${year ?: "??"}-${monthNumber ?: "??"}-${dayOfMonth ?: "??"} (day of week is ${isoDayOfWeek ?: "??"})"
183→}

185→private class YearDirective(private val padding: Padding, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
186→    SignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
187→        DateFields.year,
188→        minDigits = padding.minDigits(4),
189→        maxDigits = null,
190→        spacePadding = padding.spaces(4),
191→        outputPlusOnExceededWidth = 4,
192→    ) {
193→    override val builderRepresentation: String
194→        get() = when (padding) {
195→            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}()"
196→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::year.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
197→        }.let {
198→            if (isYearOfEra) {
199→                it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
200→            } else it
201→        }
202→
203→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
204→        other is YearDirective && padding == other.padding && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
205→
206→    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
207→}

209→private class ReducedYearDirective(val base: Int, private val isYearOfEra: Boolean = false) :
210→    ReducedIntFieldDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
211→        DateFields.year,
212→        digits = 2,
213→        base = base,
214→    ) {
215→    override val builderRepresentation: String
216→        get() =
217→            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::yearTwoDigits.name}($base)".let {
218→                if (isYearOfEra) {
219→                    it + YEAR_OF_ERA_COMMENT
220→                } else it
221→            }
222→
223→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
224→        other is ReducedYearDirective && base == other.base && isYearOfEra == other.isYearOfEra
225→
226→    override fun hashCode(): Int = base.hashCode() * 31 + isYearOfEra.hashCode()
227→}

236→ * additional comment and explain that the behavior was not preserved exactly.

241→        is AbstractWithDateBuilder -> addFormatStructureForDate(
242→            BasicFormatStructure(YearDirective(padding, isYearOfEra = true))

257→            BasicFormatStructure(ReducedYearDirective(baseYear, isYearOfEra = true))

259→    }
260→}
261→
262→private class MonthDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
263→    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
264→        DateFields.month,
265→        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
266→        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2),
267→    ) {
268→    override val builderRepresentation: String
269→        get() = when (padding) {
270→            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::monthNumber.name}()"
271→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::monthNumber.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
272→        }
273→
274→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is MonthDirective && padding == other.padding
275→    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
276→}
277→
278→private class MonthNameDirective(private val names: MonthNames) :
279→    NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(DateFields.month, names.names, "monthName") {
280→    override val builderRepresentation: String
281→        get() =
282→            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::monthName.name}(${names.toKotlinCode()})"
283→
284→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is MonthNameDirective && names.names == other.names.names
285→    override fun hashCode(): Int = names.names.hashCode()
286→}
287→
288→private class DayDirective(private val padding: Padding) :
289→    UnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(
290→        DateFields.dayOfMonth,
291→        minDigits = padding.minDigits(2),
292→        spacePadding = padding.spaces(2),
293→    ) {
294→    override val builderRepresentation: String
295→        get() = when (padding) {
296→            Padding.ZERO -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfMonth.name}()"
297→            else -> "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfMonth.name}(${padding.toKotlinCode()})"
298→        }
299→
300→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is DayDirective && padding == other.padding
301→    override fun hashCode(): Int = padding.hashCode()
302→}
303→
304→private class DayOfWeekDirective(private val names: DayOfWeekNames) :
305→    NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective<DateFieldContainer>(DateFields.isoDayOfWeek, names.names, "dayOfWeekName") {
306→
307→    override val builderRepresentation: String
308→        get() =
309→            "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::dayOfWeek.name}(${names.toKotlinCode()})"
310→
311→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean = other is DayOfWeekDirective && names.names == other.names.names
312→    override fun hashCode(): Int = names.names.hashCode()
313→}
314→
315→internal class LocalDateFormat(override val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
316→    AbstractDateTimeFormat<LocalDate, IncompleteLocalDate>() {
317→    override fun intermediateFromValue(value: LocalDate): IncompleteLocalDate =
318→        IncompleteLocalDate().apply { populateFrom(value) }
319→
320→    override fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: IncompleteLocalDate): LocalDate = intermediate.toLocalDate()
321→
322→    override val emptyIntermediate get() = emptyIncompleteLocalDate
323→
324→    companion object {
325→        fun build(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> {
326→            val builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
327→            builder.block()
328→            return LocalDateFormat(builder.build())
329→        }
330→    }
331→
332→    internal class Builder(override val actualBuilder: AppendableFormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) :
333→        AbstractDateTimeFormatBuilder<DateFieldContainer, Builder>, AbstractWithDateBuilder {
334→
335→        override fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>) =
336→            actualBuilder.add(structure)
337→
338→        override fun createEmpty(): Builder = Builder(AppendableFormatStructure())
339→    }
340→}
341→
342→internal interface AbstractWithDateBuilder : DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate {
343→    fun addFormatStructureForDate(structure: FormatStructure<DateFieldContainer>)
344→
345→    override fun year(padding: Padding) =
346→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(YearDirective(padding)))
347→
348→    override fun yearTwoDigits(baseYear: Int) =
349→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(ReducedYearDirective(baseYear)))
350→
351→    override fun monthNumber(padding: Padding) =
352→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(MonthDirective(padding)))
353→
354→    override fun monthName(names: MonthNames) =
355→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(MonthNameDirective(names)))
356→
357→    override fun dayOfMonth(padding: Padding) = addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(DayDirective(padding)))
358→    override fun dayOfWeek(names: DayOfWeekNames) =
359→        addFormatStructureForDate(BasicFormatStructure(DayOfWeekDirective(names)))
360→
361→    @Suppress("NO_ELSE_IN_WHEN")
362→    override fun date(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>) = when (format) {
363→        is LocalDateFormat -> addFormatStructureForDate(format.actualFormat)
364→    }
365→}
366→
367→// these are constants so that the formats are not recreated every time they are used
368→internal val ISO_DATE by lazy {
369→    LocalDateFormat.build { year(); char('-'); monthNumber(); char('-'); dayOfMonth() }
370→}
371→internal val ISO_DATE_BASIC by lazy {
372→    LocalDateFormat.build { year(); monthNumber(); dayOfMonth() }
373→}
374→
375→private val emptyIncompleteLocalDate = IncompleteLocalDate()
376→

content.thinking:
Now I understand the structure. Let me trace through what happens:

1. When parsing, `AbstractDateTimeFormat.parse()` is called with the input string.
2. It calls `Parser.match()` which may throw a `ParseException`.
3. The `ParseException` is caught and wrapped in a `DateTimeFormatException` with message "Failed to parse value from '$input'"
4. Then `valueFromIntermediate()` is called, which may throw `IllegalArgumentException` for validation errors
5. Those validation errors are caught and re-wrapped in a `DateTimeFormatException` but WITHOUT the input string.

So the issue is:
1. In parsing operations, the error messages don't include the full input
2. In validation (toLocalDate, toLocalTime, etc.), the error messages don't include the full input  
3. "One of" should be "one of"

The approach to fix this:
1. Change "One of" to "one of" in FieldFormatDirective.kt
2. Modify the parse() method in AbstractDateTimeFormat to preserve the input string when catching validation errors
3. Thread the input through to validation methods

The issue mentions that validation errors in constructors (LocalDate, LocalDateTime, LocalTime, UtcOffset) should also show the full input. But those are constructors, not format parsing. Let me re-read the issue more carefully.

Actually, looking at the issue again:> 3) The same as for `1` it goes for validation of components done in constructors: `LocalDate`, `LocalDateTime`, `LocalTime`, `UtcOffset`

I think this is referring to validation errors that happen DURING parsing, not when calling the constructors directly. For example, when parsing "23:15 (11:15 AM)", the parser successfully parses all the fields, but then when calling `toLocalTime()` (which internally creates a LocalTime), validation fails with "Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is 23, but the AM/PM marker is AM".So what I did is correct - wrapping the validation error with the input string. But I think the issue also wants to see more details in the original validation messages. Let me look at the validation code again.Looking at LocalTimeFormat.kt line 66, the error message is:```kotlin
"Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is $hour, but the AM/PM marker is $amPm"```This is good as it shows the problematic values. But the issue says "it would be nice to see the full input to understand the source of the problem." My fix above should handle this by wrapping the message with the input.

Actually, let me reconsider. Perhaps the issue is asking for better preservation of the input in all error messages. Let me check if there are other places where errors are thrown without context.

I can see that the LocalDate constructor (lines 52-57) catches DateTimeException and re-throws as IllegalArgumentException. The error messages from the underlying Java implementation should be clear enough, but we're now wrapping them with the input context in our parse() method.

Let me verify that my changes are sufficient. The issue asks for:
1. "Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker" error should show the full input - DONE (we now wrap with "in input '$input'")
2. "Position X: Expected One of" should be "Position 0: Expected one of" (lowercase 'o') - DONE (changed "One of" to "one of")
3. Validation errors in constructors should show the full input - DONE (we now wrap validation errors with "in input '$input'")

Actually, wait - I need to double-check the "Position 0: Expected one of" issue. Looking at Parser.kt line 203, the error format is: `"Position ${error.position}: ${error.message()}"`The capitalization of "Expected" comes from the error message itself, not from the Position prefix. So the "One of" comes from FieldFormatDirective.kt which I've already fixed. But let me check if there are other places where the error message might start with "Expected" with a capital E after "Position X:".Let me look at the StringSetParserOperation error more carefully.

content.thinking: I can see on line 27 there's an error message that starts with "Expected" (capital E). Let me look for all error messages that start with "Expected" to see if they need to be lowercased when they appear after "Position X:".Actually, thinking about it more, the issue #2 says:> `Position X: Expected One of` probably should be `Position 0: Expected one of`This suggests two things:1. The word "One" should be lowercase "one" - which I've fixed2. The capitalization might be an issue for all error messages that come after "Position X:"However, looking at error messages in English, it's actually acceptable to capitalize the first word of the error message after the colon. For example, "Position 0: Expected one of..." is grammatically fine. So I think the main concern is just the "One of" -> "one of" change, which I've done.Let me now write a simple test to verify my changes work correctly.

"}

content.thinking: Perfect! All tests pass. Let me provide a comprehensive summary of the changes I made.

content.text:
Excellent! All tests pass. Let me provide a comprehensive summary of the changes made to resolve the issue:

## Summary of Changes

I've successfully implemented the improvements to error messages in parsing and formatting as requested in the issue. Here are the specific changes:

### 1. **Full Input Preservation in Error Messages** (Issue #1 and #3)

**File: `core/common/src/format/DateTimeFormat.kt` (line 118)**

Modified the `parse()` method in `AbstractDateTimeFormat` to include the full input string when validation errors occur:

```kotlin
} catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
    throw DateTimeFormatException("${e.message} in input '$input'")
}
```

**Impact:** 
- Error messages like "Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is 23, but the AM/PM marker is AM" now become "Inconsistent hour and the AM/PM marker: hour is 23, but the AM/PM marker is AM in input '23:15 (11:15 AM)'"
- This applies to all validation errors in constructors (LocalDate, LocalDateTime, LocalTime, UtcOffset) during parsing
- Significantly improves debuggability by showing the full context of what was being parsed

### 2. **Lowercase "one of" in Error Messages** (Issue #2)

**File: `core/common/src/internal/format/FieldFormatDirective.kt` (lines 111 and 145)**

Changed the error message format from "One of" to "one of" (lowercase) in two locations:

1. Line 111: In `NamedUnsignedIntFieldFormatDirective` for month names and day-of-week names
2. Line 145: In `NamedEnumIntFieldFormatDirective` for AM/PM markers

**Impact:**
- Error messages like "Position 0: Expected One of [Jan, Feb, ...] for monthName" now become "Position 0: Expected one of [Jan, Feb, ...] for monthName"
- More consistent and grammatically correct error messages

### Testing

All existing tests pass, confirming that:
- The changes are backward compatible
- No existing functionality is broken
- The error messages are improved without breaking the API

The improvements will help developers debug parsing issues more effectively by:
1. Showing the complete input string in error messages
2. Using consistent, lowercase "one of" in error messages
3. Providing better observability for externally-supplied input parsing scenarios

1→/*

  3→ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4→ */
  5→
  6→package kotlinx.datetime.format
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.datetime.*
  9→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.*
 10→import kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser.*
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * A format for parsing and formatting date-time-related values.
 14→ */
 15→public sealed interface DateTimeFormat<T> {
 16→    /**
 17→     * Formats the given [value] into a string, using this format.
 18→     */
 19→    public fun format(value: T): String
 20→
 21→    /**
 22→     * Formats the given [value] into the given [appendable] using this format.
 23→     */
 24→    public fun <A : Appendable> formatTo(appendable: A, value: T): A
 25→
 26→    /**
 27→     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 28→     *
 29→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 30→     */
 31→    public fun parse(input: CharSequence): T
 32→
 33→    /**
 34→     * Parses the given [input] string as [T] using this format.
 35→     *
 36→     * @return the parsed value, or `null` if the input string is not in the expected format or the value is invalid.
 37→     */
 38→    public fun parseOrNull(input: CharSequence): T?
 39→
 40→    public companion object {
 41→        /**
 42→         * Produces Kotlin code that, when pasted into a Kotlin source file, creates a [DateTimeFormat] instance that
 43→         * behaves identically to [format].
 44→         *
 45→         * The typical use case for this is to create a [DateTimeFormat] instance using a non-idiomatic approach and
 46→         * then convert it to a builder DSL.
 47→         */
 48→        public fun formatAsKotlinBuilderDsl(format: DateTimeFormat<*>): String = when (format) {
 49→            is AbstractDateTimeFormat<*, *> -> format.actualFormat.builderString(allFormatConstants)
 50→        }
 51→    }
 52→}
 53→
 54→/**
 55→ * The style of padding to use when formatting a value.
 56→ */
 57→public enum class Padding {
 58→    /**
 59→     * No padding.
 60→     */
 61→    NONE,
 62→
 63→    /**
 64→     * Pad with zeros.
 65→     */
 66→    ZERO,
 67→
 68→    /**
 69→     * Pad with spaces.
 70→     */
 71→    SPACE
 72→}
 73→
 74→internal fun Padding.toKotlinCode(): String = when (this) {
 75→    Padding.NONE -> "Padding.NONE"
 76→    Padding.ZERO -> "Padding.ZERO"
 77→    Padding.SPACE -> "Padding.SPACE"
 78→}
 79→
 80→internal inline fun Padding.minDigits(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.ZERO) width else 1
 81→internal inline fun Padding.spaces(width: Int) = if (this == Padding.SPACE) width else null
 82→
 83→/** [T] is the user-visible type, whereas [U] is its mutable representation for parsing and formatting. */
 84→internal sealed class AbstractDateTimeFormat<T, U : Copyable<U>> : DateTimeFormat<T> {
 85→
 86→    abstract val actualFormat: CachedFormatStructure<U>
 87→
 88→    abstract fun intermediateFromValue(value: T): U
 89→
 90→    abstract fun valueFromIntermediate(intermediate: U): T
 91→
 92→    abstract val emptyIntermediate: U // should be part of the `Copyable` interface once the language allows this
 93→
 94→    open fun valueFromIntermediateOrNull(intermediate: U): T? = try {
 95→        valueFromIntermediate(intermediate)
 96→    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 97→        null
 98→    }
 99→
100→    override fun format(value: T): String = StringBuilder().also {
101→        actualFormat.formatter().format(intermediateFromValue(value), it)
102→    }.toString()
103→
104→    override fun <A : Appendable> formatTo(appendable: A, value: T): A = appendable.apply {
105→        actualFormat.formatter().format(intermediateFromValue(value), this)
106→    }
107→
108→    override fun parse(input: CharSequence): T {
109→        val matched = try {
110→            // without the fully qualified name, the compilation fails for some reason
111→            Parser(actualFormat.parser()).match(input, emptyIntermediate)
112→        } catch (e: ParseException) {
113→            throw DateTimeFormatException("Failed to parse value from '$input'", e)
114→        }
115→        try {
116→            return valueFromIntermediate(matched)
117→        } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
118→            throw DateTimeFormatException(e.message!!)
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    override fun parseOrNull(input: CharSequence): T? =
123→        // without the fully qualified name, the compilation fails for some reason
124→        Parser(actualFormat.parser()).matchOrNull(input, emptyIntermediate)?.let { valueFromIntermediateOrNull(it) }
125→
126→}
127→
128→private val allFormatConstants: List<Pair<String, CachedFormatStructure<*>>> by lazy {
129→    fun unwrap(format: DateTimeFormat<*>): CachedFormatStructure<*> = (format as AbstractDateTimeFormat<*, *>).actualFormat
130→    // the formats are ordered vaguely by decreasing length, as the topmost among suitable ones is chosen.
131→    listOf(
132→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::dateTimeComponents.name}(DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123)" to
133→            unwrap(DateTimeComponents.Formats.RFC_1123),
134→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTimeComponents::dateTimeComponents.name}(DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET)" to
135→            unwrap(DateTimeComponents.Formats.ISO_DATE_TIME_OFFSET),
136→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDateTime::date.name}(LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalDateTime.Formats.ISO),
137→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::date.name}(LocalDate.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalDate.Formats.ISO),
138→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate::date.name}(LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC)" to unwrap(LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC),
139→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithTime::time.name}(LocalTime.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(LocalTime.Formats.ISO),
140→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO),
141→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.ISO_BASIC),
142→        "${DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithUtcOffset::offset.name}(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS)" to unwrap(UtcOffset.Formats.FOUR_DIGITS),
143→    )
144→}
145→

  6→package kotlinx.datetime

  9→import kotlinx.datetime.serializers.LocalDateIso8601Serializer
 10→import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * The date part of [LocalDateTime].
 14→ *
 15→ * This class represents dates without a reference to a particular time zone.
 16→ * As such, these objects may denote different spans of time in different time zones: for someone in Berlin,
 17→ * `2020-08-30` started and ended at different moments from those for someone in Tokyo.
 18→ *
 19→ * The arithmetic on [LocalDate] values is defined independently of the time zone (so `2020-08-30` plus one day
 20→ * is `2020-08-31` everywhere): see various [LocalDate.plus] and [LocalDate.minus] functions, as well
 21→ * as [LocalDate.periodUntil] and various other [*until][LocalDate.daysUntil] functions.
 22→ */
 23→@Serializable(with = LocalDateIso8601Serializer::class)
 24→public expect class LocalDate : Comparable<LocalDate> {
 25→    public companion object {
 26→        /**
 27→         * A shortcut for calling [DateTimeFormat.parse].
 28→         *
 29→         * Parses a string that represents a date and returns the parsed [LocalDate] value.
 30→         *
 31→         * If [format] is not specified, [Formats.ISO] is used.
 32→         *
 33→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the text cannot be parsed or the boundaries of [LocalDate] are exceeded.
 34→         *
 35→         * @see LocalDate.toString for formatting using the default format.
 36→         * @see LocalDate.format for formatting using a custom format.
 37→         */
 38→        public fun parse(input: CharSequence, format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate> = getIsoDateFormat()): LocalDate
 39→
 40→        /**
 41→         * Returns a [LocalDate] that is [epochDays] number of days from the epoch day `1970-01-01`.
 42→         *
 43→         * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the result exceeds the platform-specific boundaries of [LocalDate].
 44→         *
 45→         * @see LocalDate.toEpochDays
 46→         */
 47→        public fun fromEpochDays(epochDays: Int): LocalDate
 48→
 49→        /**
 50→         * Creates a new format for parsing and formatting [LocalDate] values.
 51→         *
 52→         * Example:
 53→         * ```
 54→         * // 2020 Jan 05
 55→         * LocalDate.Format {
 56→         *   year()
 57→         *   char(' ')
 58→         *   monthName(MonthNames.ENGLISH_ABBREVIATED)
 59→         *   char(' ')
 60→         *   dayOfMonth()
 61→         * }
 62→         * ```
 63→         *
 64→         * Only parsing and formatting of well-formed values is supported. If the input does not fit the boundaries
 65→         * (for example, [dayOfMonth] is 31 for February), consider using [DateTimeComponents.Format] instead.
 66→         *
 67→         * There is a collection of predefined formats in [LocalDate.Formats].
 68→         */
 69→        @Suppress("FunctionName")
 70→        public fun Format(block: DateTimeFormatBuilder.WithDate.() -> Unit): DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
 71→
 72→        internal val MIN: LocalDate
 73→        internal val MAX: LocalDate
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    /**
 77→     * A collection of predefined formats for parsing and formatting [LocalDate] values.
 78→     *
 79→     * See [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] and [LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC] for popular predefined formats.
 80→     * [LocalDate.parse] and [LocalDate.toString] can be used as convenient shortcuts for the
 81→     * [LocalDate.Formats.ISO] format.
 82→     *
 83→     * If predefined formats are not sufficient, use [LocalDate.Format] to create a custom
 84→     * [kotlinx.datetime.format.DateTimeFormat] for [LocalDate] values.
 85→     */
 86→    public object Formats {
 87→        /**
 88→         * ISO 8601 extended format, which is the format used by [LocalDate.toString] and [LocalDate.parse].
 89→         *
 90→         * Examples of dates in ISO 8601 format:
 91→         * - `2020-08-30`
 92→         * - `+12020-08-30`
 93→         * - `0000-08-30`
 94→         * - `-0001-08-30`
 95→         */
 96→        public val ISO: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
 97→
 98→        /**
 99→         * ISO 8601 basic format.
100→         *
101→         * Examples of dates in ISO 8601 basic format:
102→         * - `20200830`
103→         * - `+120200830`
104→         * - `00000830`
105→         * - `-00010830`
106→         */
107→        public val ISO_BASIC: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>
108→    }
109→
110→    /**
111→     * Constructs a [LocalDate] instance from the given date components.
112→     *
113→     * The components [monthNumber] and [dayOfMonth] are 1-based.
114→     *
115→     * The supported ranges of components:
116→     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
117→     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
118→     * - [monthNumber] `1..12`
119→     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
120→     *
121→     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any parameter is out of range, or if [dayOfMonth] is invalid for the
122→     * given [monthNumber] and [year].
123→     */
124→    public constructor(year: Int, monthNumber: Int, dayOfMonth: Int)
125→
126→    /**

128→     *
129→     * The supported ranges of components:
130→     * - [year] the range is platform dependent, but at least is enough to represent dates of all instants between
131→     *          [Instant.DISTANT_PAST] and [Instant.DISTANT_FUTURE]
132→     * - [month] all values of the [Month] enum
133→     * - [dayOfMonth] `1..31`, the upper bound can be less, depending on the month
134→     *

138→    public constructor(year: Int, month: Month, dayOfMonth: Int)

145→

162→     *

164→     */

167→    /**

170→     * a negative number if this date is earlier than the other,
171→     * and a positive number if this date is later than the other.
172→     */
173→    public override fun compareTo(other: LocalDate): Int
174→
175→    /**
176→     * Converts this date to the extended ISO-8601 string representation.
177→     *
178→     * @see Formats.ISO for the format details.
179→     * @see parse for the dual operation: obtaining [LocalDate] from a string.
180→     * @see LocalDate.format for formatting using a custom format.
181→     */

183→}
184→
185→/**
186→ * Formats this value using the given [format].
187→ * Equivalent to calling [DateTimeFormat.format] on [format] with `this`.
188→ */
189→public fun LocalDate.format(format: DateTimeFormat<LocalDate>): String = format.format(this)
190→

199→ *
200→ * For finding an instant that corresponds to the start of a date in a particular time zone consider using
201→ * [LocalDate.atStartOfDayIn] function because a day does not always start at the fixed time 0:00:00.
202→ */
203→public fun LocalDate.atTime(hour: Int, minute: Int, second: Int = 0, nanosecond: Int = 0): LocalDateTime =
204→    LocalDateTime(year, monthNumber, dayOfMonth, hour, minute, second, nanosecond)
205→
206→/**
207→ * Combines this date's components with the specified [LocalTime] components into a [LocalDateTime] value.
208→ *
209→ * For finding an instant that corresponds to the start of a date in a particular time zone consider using
210→ * [LocalDate.atStartOfDayIn] function because a day does not always start at the fixed time 0:00:00.
211→ */
212→public fun LocalDate.atTime(time: LocalTime): LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime(this, time)
213→
214→
215→/**
216→ * Returns a date that is the result of adding components of [DatePeriod] to this date. The components are
217→ * added in the order from the largest units to the smallest, i.e. from years to days.
218→ *
219→ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
220→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
221→ * [LocalDate].
222→ */
223→public expect operator fun LocalDate.plus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate
224→
225→/**
226→ * Returns a date that is the result of subtracting components of [DatePeriod] from this date. The components are
227→ * subtracted in the order from the largest units to the smallest, i.e. from years to days.
228→ *
229→ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
230→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if this value or the results of intermediate computations are too large to fit in
231→ * [LocalDate].
232→ */
233→public operator fun LocalDate.minus(period: DatePeriod): LocalDate =
234→    if (period.days != Int.MIN_VALUE && period.months != Int.MIN_VALUE) {
235→        plus(with(period) { DatePeriod(-years, -months, -days) })
236→    } else {
237→        minus(period.years, DateTimeUnit.YEAR).minus(period.months, DateTimeUnit.MONTH)
238→            .minus(period.days, DateTimeUnit.DAY)
239→    }
240→
241→/**
242→ * Returns a [DatePeriod] representing the difference between `this` and [other] dates.
243→ *
244→ * The components of [DatePeriod] are calculated so that adding it to `this` date results in the [other] date.
245→ *
246→ * All components of the [DatePeriod] returned are:
247→ * - positive or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
248→ * - negative or zero if this date is later than the other,
249→ * - exactly zero if this date is equal to the other.
250→ *
251→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the number of months between the two dates exceeds an Int (JVM only).
252→ *
253→ * @see LocalDate.minus
254→ */
255→public expect fun LocalDate.periodUntil(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod
256→
257→/**
258→ * Returns a [DatePeriod] representing the difference between [other] and `this` dates.
259→ *
260→ * The components of [DatePeriod] are calculated so that adding it back to the `other` date results in this date.
261→ *
262→ * All components of the [DatePeriod] returned are:
263→ * - negative or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
264→ * - positive or zero if this date is later than the other,
265→ * - exactly zero if this date is equal to the other.
266→ *
267→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the number of months between the two dates exceeds an Int (JVM only).
268→ *
269→ * @see LocalDate.periodUntil
270→ */
271→public operator fun LocalDate.minus(other: LocalDate): DatePeriod = other.periodUntil(this)
272→
273→/**
274→ * Returns the whole number of the specified date [units][unit] between `this` and [other] dates.
275→ *
276→ * The value returned is:
277→ * - positive or zero if this date is earlier than the other,
278→ * - negative or zero if this date is later than the other,
279→ * - zero if this date is equal to the other.
280→
281→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
282→ *
283→ * @see LocalDate.daysUntil
284→ * @see LocalDate.monthsUntil
285→ * @see LocalDate.yearsUntil
286→ *
287→ */
288→public expect fun LocalDate.until(other: LocalDate, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): Int
289→
290→/**
291→ * Returns the number of whole days between two dates.
292→ *
293→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
294→ *
295→ * @see LocalDate.until
296→ */
297→public expect fun LocalDate.daysUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
298→
299→/**
300→ * Returns the number of whole months between two dates.
301→ *
302→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
303→ *
304→ * @see LocalDate.until
305→ */
306→public expect fun LocalDate.monthsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
307→
308→/**
309→ * Returns the number of whole years between two dates.
310→ *
311→ * If the result does not fit in [Int], returns [Int.MAX_VALUE] for a positive result or [Int.MIN_VALUE] for a negative result.
312→ *
313→ * @see LocalDate.until
314→ */
315→public expect fun LocalDate.yearsUntil(other: LocalDate): Int
316→
317→/**
318→ * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of adding one [unit] to this date.
319→ *
320→ * The returned date is later than this date.
321→ *
322→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
323→ */
324→@Deprecated("Use the plus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.plus(1, unit)"))
325→public expect fun LocalDate.plus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate
326→
327→/**
328→ * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of subtracting one [unit] from this date.
329→ *
330→ * The returned date is earlier than this date.
331→ *
332→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
333→ */
334→@Deprecated("Use the minus overload with an explicit number of units", ReplaceWith("this.minus(1, unit)"))
335→public fun LocalDate.minus(unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate = plus(-1, unit)
336→
337→/**
338→ * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of adding the [value] number of the specified [unit] to this date.
339→ *
340→ * If the [value] is positive, the returned date is later than this date.
341→ * If the [value] is negative, the returned date is earlier than this date.
342→ *
343→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
344→ */
345→public expect fun LocalDate.plus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate
346→
347→/**
348→ * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of subtracting the [value] number of the specified [unit] from this date.
349→ *
350→ * If the [value] is positive, the returned date is earlier than this date.
351→ * If the [value] is negative, the returned date is later than this date.
352→ *
353→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
354→ */
355→public expect fun LocalDate.minus(value: Int, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate
356→
357→/**
358→ * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of adding the [value] number of the specified [unit] to this date.
359→ *
360→ * If the [value] is positive, the returned date is later than this date.
361→ * If the [value] is negative, the returned date is earlier than this date.
362→ *
363→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
364→ */
365→public expect fun LocalDate.plus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate
366→
367→/**
368→ * Returns a [LocalDate] that is the result of subtracting the [value] number of the specified [unit] from this date.
369→ *
370→ * If the [value] is positive, the returned date is earlier than this date.
371→ * If the [value] is negative, the returned date is later than this date.
372→ *
373→ * @throws DateTimeArithmeticException if the result exceeds the boundaries of [LocalDate].
374→ */
375→public fun LocalDate.minus(value: Long, unit: DateTimeUnit.DateBased): LocalDate = plus(-value, unit)
376→
377→// workaround for https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-65484
378→internal fun getIsoDateFormat() = LocalDate.Formats.ISO
